The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[ja:手長神社]] ]]

It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]]

revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tenaga Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:not stubs]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[ja:手長神社]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}
