{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} [[Category:Suwa City History]] There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden

{{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa {{Cite web|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == Ashinaga|ja|手長足長}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線|12=draft}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=draft}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenaga Shrine]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[en:Tenaga Shrine]] [[sn:enaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}}

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線|12=draft}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=draft}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenaga Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenaga Shrine]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[en:Tenaga Shrine]] [[sn:enaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}}

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=draft|12=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=draft|12=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=draft|12=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=draft|12=draft}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenaga Shrine]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[en:Tenaga Shrine]] [[sn:enaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}}

revision.comment: 36 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:手長神社 up to rev 102002942
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 手長神社 |画像 = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[諏訪市]]上諏訪茶臼山9556 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|02|44.9|N|138|07|15.3|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=手長神社}} |祭神 = [[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|手摩乳命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]]
[[諏訪大社]]上社末社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月15日]] |神事 = 相撲神事 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=手長神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|02|44.9|N|138|07|15.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|02|44.9|N|138|07|15.3|E}}}} }} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|一の鳥居]] '''手長神社'''（てながじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]上諏訪にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。[[諏訪大社]]上社末社。 == 祭神 == * '''[[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|手摩乳命]]''' （てなづちのみこと） :: 別名を「手長彦神」といい、[[諏訪大社]]の祭神・[[建御名方神]]に随従する神。建御名方神が諏訪大社に祀られる以前からこの地で信仰されていた神とされる。 :: [[日本神話]]では、建御名方神の先祖の[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫]]の母神の名として登場する。同じ諏訪市内には父神の名である足摩乳命を祀る[[足長神社]]もある。 == 歴史 == 創建の由諸は不明であるが、境内の近くには旧石器時代・古墳時代の複合遺跡である手長丘遺跡が、境内上方には茶臼山古墳群があり、周辺には古代から人が住んでいた[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html　諏訪市「諏訪市の旧石器時代と黒曜石」]。 古くは「手長宮」・「手長大明神」と称され、諏訪大社の末社であった。 元は桑原郷[http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/busi/bu.htm 車山高原レア・メモリーが語る諏訪武士の誕生史１５）桑原氏]の総鎮守で、足摩乳命とともに祀られていた。のちに鎌倉時代に桑原郷が上桑原と下桑原に分けられたとき、下桑原に手摩乳命を祀る手長神社が作られ、上桑原の足長神社とともにそれぞれの鎮守となったとされる。 領主・武家や庶民からの崇敬が篤く、[[高島城]]の[[鬼門]]に位置することから[[諏訪藩]]家中の総鎮守とされた。現在の社殿は[[天明]]年間に[[立川和四郎|立川和四郎富棟]]が建築したが、昭和27年（1952年）に半焼し、修理された。 == 境内 == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|本殿
[[神明造]]。 File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|参道と拝殿
拝殿は市指定文化財。 File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|回廊 File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|三の鳥居 == 摂末社 == 拝殿横に多くの摂末社が鎮座している。 File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|彌榮神社（市指定文化財）
本社旧本殿。 == 主な祭事 == * 八朔祭 ** 夕祭 （[[9月14日]]） ** 例祭 （[[9月15日]]） ** 二の祭 （[[9月16日]]） == 文化財 == === 諏訪市指定文化財 === * 拝殿（有形文化財） - 昭和59年指定 * 旧本殿（有形文化財） - 平成6年指定 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[長野県]][[諏訪市]]茶臼山9556 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[中央本線]] [[上諏訪駅]] （徒歩約7分） 　　　　　　上諏訪駅の東側の高台に位置し、諏訪湖を見下ろすことができる。 == 参考文献 == * 『日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）諏訪市 手長神社項 *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * [[足長神社]] （諏訪市四賀普門寺） * [[八剣神社 (諏訪市)|八剣神社]] （諏訪市小和田） * [[手長足長]] * [[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ]] * [[上諏訪町]] * [[諏訪市#神社|諏訪市の神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html 信濃國諏訪郡下桑原郷鎮守 手長神社] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ 手長神社・Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official 手長神社・Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]　[[諏訪信仰]]と[[神仏習合]]と[[神仏分離]]の歴史のプロジェクト {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てなかしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]]

{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa City History]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suwa City]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tenaga Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:not stubs]] [[Category:broken nos]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tenaga Shrine]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[en:Tenaga Shrine]] [[sn:enaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tenaga Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|手長神社|version=102002942|comment=Imported full ja history}}

There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|qq=}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = [[File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}}} | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qq=}} (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qq=}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|qq=}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|qq=}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kamisuwa Station|ja|上諏訪駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō Main Line|qq=}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|qq=}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|qq=}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|qq=}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|qq=}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] -
