[[commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|qid=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|qid=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * [[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|qq=|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * [[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - [[commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * [[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall

It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * [[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|手長神社|version=102002942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{Commons category|Tenaga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11497169}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''[[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: [[:en:Kami-Suwa Station|Kamisuwa Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * [[:en:Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|手長神社|version=102002942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{Commons category|Tenaga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11497169}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11497169]] Wikidata name: Tenaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)

The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kami-Suwa Station|en|Kami-Suwa Station|WD=Q844049|lt=Kamisuwa Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - A project exploring the history of Suwa faith and the separation and unification of Shinto and Buddhism == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|手長神社|version=102002942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine]] [[ja:手長神社]] {{Commons category|Tenaga-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11497169}} [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenaga-jinja]][[ja:手長神社]] {{Cite web|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:18 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenaga Shrine | image = File:Tenaga jinja, Haiden.jpg | location = Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture | lat_degrees = 36 | lat_minutes = 02 | lat_seconds = 44.92 | long_degrees = 138 | long_minutes = 07 | long_seconds = 15.32 | deity = {{ill|Tenazuchi|WD=Q109554669}} | object = | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine, Subordinate Shrine of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} Upper Shrine | established = Unknown | architecture = Shinmei-zukuri | nickname = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 15 | rituals = Sumo Ritual | location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.045811|N|138.120922|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} {{maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{coord|36|02|44.92|N|138|07|15.32|E}}|marker=point}} [[File:Tenaga-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|First Torii]] '''Tenaga Shrine''' (手長神社, Tenaga Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamisuwa, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. It was formerly classified as a Prefectural Shrine and is now a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa Taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == deity == * '''{{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}}''' (Tenazuchi no Mikoto) :: Also known as Tenaga-hiko no Kami, this deity accompanies {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, the main deity of Suwa Taisha. It is believed that Tenazuchi no Mikoto was worshipped in this area before Takeminakata was enshrined at Suwa Taisha. :: In Japanese mythology, Tenazuchi no Mikoto is the mother of {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, who is an ancestor of Takeminakata. There is also a shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|ja|アシナヅチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシナヅチ・テナヅチ}}, her husband, in Suwa City. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown, but nearby are the Tenaga Hill Ruins, a complex site from the Paleolithic and Kofun periods, and the Chausuyama Kofun Cluster. This area has been inhabited since ancient times[https://www.city.suwa.lg.jp/soshiki/28/2165.html Suwa City "The Paleolithic Period and Obsidian in Suwa City"]. Historically, the shrine was known as Tenaga-no-Miya or Tenaga Daimyojin and was a subordinate shrine of Suwa Taisha. Originally, it was the general guardian of the Kuwabara-go district, enshrining Ashinazuchi no Mikoto alongside Tenazuchi no Mikoto. During the Kamakura period, when Kuwabara-go was divided into Kamikuwabara and Shimokuwabara, Tenazuchi no Mikoto was enshrined in Shimokuwabara, becoming the guardian of the lower Kuwabara district, while Ashinazuchi no Mikoto remained the guardian of Kamikuwabara. The shrine has long been revered by local lords, warriors, and commoners, and was considered the general guardian of the Suwa domain due to its location in the northeast, the demon gate (kimon) direction of Takashima Castle. The current shrine building was constructed by {{ill|Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja|立川和四郎富棟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa Washiro Tomimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the Tenmei era, but it was partially destroyed by fire in 1952 (Showa 27) and subsequently repaired. == Shrine Grounds == File:Tenaga-jinja honden.JPG|main hall
Shinmei-zukuri style File:Tenaga-jinja sandou.JPG|Approach and Haiden
The Haiden is a city-designated cultural property. File:Tenaga-jinja kairou.JPG|Corridor File:Tenaga-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kagura Hall File:Tenaga-jinja torii.JPG|Third Torii == Subsidiary Shrines == Several subsidiary shrines are located next to the Haiden. File:Tenaga-jinja honden (old).JPG|Yasaka Shrine (city-designated cultural property)
Former main hall of Tenaga Shrine == Main festivals == * Hachisaku festival ** Evening festival (September 14) ** Main festival (September 15) ** Second festival (September 16) == Cultural Properties == === Suwa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Haiden (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Showa 59 (1984) * Former main hall (Tangible Cultural Property) - designated in Heisei 6 (1994) == Visitor Information == ; location * Kamisuwa Chausuyama 9556, Suwa City, Nagano Prefecture ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Kami-Suwa Station|en|Kami-Suwa Station|WD=Q844049|lt=Kamisuwa Station|1=Kami-Suwa Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} (JR East) (about 7 minutes on foot). The shrine is located on a hill to the east of Kamisuwa Station, overlooking Lake Suwa. == References == * {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja|日本歴史地名大系 長野県の地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Nagano-ken no Chimei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Heibonsha) Suwa City, Tenaga Shrine entry * {{ill|Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja|谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃'|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Gods - Shrines and Holy Places - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2000, Hakusuisha) == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Tenaga-jinja}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinazuchi Shrine|1=Ashinaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637062}} (Suwa City, Shiga Fumonji) * {{ill|Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City, Kowata)|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)|qq=}} (Suwa City, Kowata) * {{ill|Tenaga and Ashinaga|ja|手長足長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga and Ashinaga|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|en|Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|1=Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi}} * {{ill|Kamisuwa|ja|上諏訪町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa|1=Kamisuwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17176357}} * {{ill|Suwa City#Shrines|ja|諏訪市の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City#Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.lcv.ne.jp/~tenaga/index.html Shinano Province Suwa District Shimosuwabara-go Guardian Tenaga Shrine] * [https://m.facebook.com/%E6%89%8B%E9%95%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E9%95%B7%E9%87%8E%E7%9C%8C%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E5%B8%82-106892784499587/ Tenaga Shrine on Facebook] * [https://mobile.twitter.com/tenaga_official Tenaga Shrine on Twitter] * {{Instagram|tenagajinja}} * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Shinto-Buddhist Project] -
